hook_type: Verbatim investor quote opener (peer signal at parity with experts, fact:authority-edelman-2024-peer-parity)
tactic: Peer-investor quote -> fund stack -> first-person recursion
primary_text:
"I've looked at Birchal from the founder's seat and the investor's seat, and from both angles the answer is the same. Birchal is one of the most important companies in Australian fintech."

Dom Pym, Triple Bubble.

Triple Bubble's first investment from their new fund. Australia Venture Partners Fund and Tribe Global have followed.

Now we're opening our own raise.

Save your spot. Read the offer when it opens.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: Founder seat, investor seat, same answer
description: Why Triple Bubble, AVPF, and Tribe Global are backing us.
_source_label: DRC V2 -- B2 Investor-validation / Peer-decision (existing investors)
_v2_changes: Subject discipline: Pym quote treats Birchal as a company (correct). Then 'we're opening our own raise' = same Birchal. No platform/company swap.

Length: 79 words (down from 100). Most of the cut came from removing 'The funds spending their days inside Australian fintech think Birchal is the platform' (paraphrase of QUOTE-002 that re-introduced the platform/company tension) and the redundant cadence-of-three platform stats line.
Description: 'are backing Birchal' -> 'are backing us' to stay in first-person voice.
'Co-founder Up. Triple Bubble.' attribution simplified to 'Triple Bubble' alone -- Pym's main affiliation in this context.
_drc_playbook: 11 Lifecycle Email Promo / Launch (B2C considered, most-aware, active)
_drc_audience: B2C considered, most-aware. Existing Birchal investors who understand CSF mechanics; peer signal weighs more than platform stats.
_compliance_findings_resolved: PREOFFER-VERBS: 'save your spot', 'read'. No transaction verbs.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'Read the offer when it opens'.
QUOTE-001: Dom Pym pasted verbatim from facts.json.
Fund attribution from QUOTE-003 (Don McKenzie / Tribe Global) and QUOTE-004 (Shayne Gary / AVPF). Verbatim or near-verbatim phrasing.
